parameters.errorMessage: Missing required field 'email' on incoming order.

parameters.text: =✅ Win back incomplete checkouts with HTTP / REST API and Telegram and lo — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Hourly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 6 h. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Read Pending Queue (Google Sheets)

### Read Pending Queue (Google Sheets)

googleSheets — `read` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Required Fields Present? · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Required Fields Present?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.email }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Work in Batches · ②→ Abort — Malformed Input

### Abort — Malformed Input

Fails fast with a clear message; triggers the error workflow instead of processing bad data.

### Work in Batches

Loop controller — releases 20 item(s) per pass. Output 2 (loop) processes the batch, output 1 (done) fires after the last batch.

Ports: ①→ Aggregate Batch Results · ②→ Process Batch Item

### Process Batch Item

POST https://api.example.com/v1/orders — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Respect Rate Limits · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Respect Rate Limits

Pauses 2 seconds — spacing calls to respect rate limits.

Next: Work in Batches

### Aggregate Batch Results

Collapses all items into a single summary item (for counts and run stats).

Next: Mark Completed in Google Sheets

### Mark Completed in Google Sheets

googleSheets — `appendOrUpdate` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Save Execution Metadata · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

nodes.name: Note: Mark Completed in Google Sheets

parameters.content:
### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Cap Batch Volume

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 25 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Win back incomplete checkouts with HTTP / REST API and Telegram and log the results

**Pattern:** Abandoned Cart Recovery · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Win back incomplete checkouts with HTTP / REST API and Telegram and log the results. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual order ops is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Hourly Schedule → Read Pending Queue (Google Sheets) → Required Fields Present? → Work in Batches → Aggregate Batch Results → Mark Completed in Google Sheets → Save Execution Metadata → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to abandoned cart recovery without manual effort
- Anyone automating order ops in e-commerce
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Improves response time
- Keeps your systems in sync

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## ⏱ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait — throughput drops, reliability rises.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your order ops process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
